name: Classic White Stock
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the chicken bones and wing tips well.
2. Place the bones into a stockpot and cover with water. Bring to a boil, then drain and rinse.
3. Return the bones to the stockpot and cover with 12 quarts cold water. Place over a medium flame.
4. Bring barely to a boil, reduce heat to a gentle simmer, and skim carefully.
5. Tie the bay leaf, thyme, peppercorns, parsley, and cloves in cheesecloth.
6. Add the mirepoix (onion, carrot, celery) and sachet to the stockpot.
7. Simmer gently for 3-4 hours, adding water as necessary to cover the bones.
8. Strain through a chinois or cheesecloth into a clean container.
9. Cool quickly in an ice bath.
10. Cover and refrigerate until chilled.
11. Remove and discard any accumulated fat from the top of the chilled stock.
12. Return to low heat and reduce as needed.
Note: Variations include using veal, beef, lamb, or turkey bones. When using beef or veal bones, simmer for 6-8 hours; when using turkey or lamb bones, simmer for 3-4 hours.
